name: repid

metadata.name: repid
metadata.summary: Repid framework: simple to use, fast to run and extensible to adopt job scheduler

documentation:
# repid [![PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/repid.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/repid/) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/aleksul/repid/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=IP3Z1VXB1G)](https://codecov.io/gh/aleksul/repid) [![Tests](https://github.com/aleksul/repid/actions/workflows/tests.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/aleksul/repid/actions/workflows/tests.yaml) [![PyPI pyversions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/repid.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/repid/) [![Read documentation](https://img.shields.io/badge/read-documentation-informational.svg)](https://repid.aleksul.space)
**Repid** framework: simple to use, fast to run and extensible to adopt job scheduler.
```bash pip install repid ``` ## Quickstart Here is how the easiest example of producer-consumer application can look like. Producer: ```python import asyncio from repid import Connection, Job, RabbitMessageBroker, Repid app = Repid(Connection(RabbitMessageBroker("amqp://user:password@localhost:5672"))) async def main() -> None: async with app.magic(): await Job(name="awesome_job").enqueue() asyncio.run(main()) ``` Consumer: ```python import asyncio from repid import Connection, RabbitMessageBroker, Repid, Router, Worker app = Repid(Connection(RabbitMessageBroker("amqp://user:password@localhost:5672"))) router = Router() @router.actor async def awesome_job() -> None: print("Hello async jobs!") await asyncio.sleep(1.0) async def main() -> None: async with app.magic(): await Worker(routers=[router]).run() asyncio.run(main()) ``` Check out [user guide] to learn more! ## License **Repid** is distributed under the terms of the MIT license. Please see [License.md] for more information. **Repid's logo** is distributed under the terms of the [CC BY-NC 4.0] license. It is originally created by [ari_the_crow_]. [License.md]: https://github.com/aleksul/repid/blob/master/LICENSE [user guide]: https://repid.aleksul.space [CC BY-NC 4.0]: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/ [ari_the_crow_]: https://www.instagram.com/p/Cd-ob1NNZ84/

functions.name: repid.connections.rabbitmq.utils.durable_message_decider
documentation: Decides if queue is set as durable in RabbitMQ.

functions.name: repid.connections.rabbitmq.utils.qnc
documentation: Queue name constructor for RabbitMQ.

classes.name: repid.connections.rabbitmq.utils._Consumers
documentation:
A special dictionary which informs Repid's RabbitMQ consumer
that aiormq has removed consumer's callback from the channel.

functions.name: repid.connections.redis.utils.get_priorities_order
documentation:
Randomizes the order in which priorities should be processed,
based on the supplied chances.

Args:
    priorities_chanses (List[float]): list of floats, representing chances, e.g.
    `[0.6, 0.3, 0.1]`.

Returns:
    List[PrioritiesT]: 3 priorities in order of processing.

functions.name: repid.connections.redis.utils.parse_priorities_distribution
documentation:
Turns priorities distribution string into list of floats.

Args:
    priorities_distribution (str): distribution of priorities, from highest to lowest, e.g.
    `10/3/1`.

Raises:
    ValueError: if priorities_distribution does not match `VALID_PRIORITIES` regex.

Returns:
    List[float]: list of 3 floats, representing chances, e.g. `[0.6, 0.3, 0.1]`.

functions.name: repid.connections.redis.utils.qnc
documentation:
Queue name constructor.

Args:
    queue_name (str): name of the queue
    priority (int, optional): priority of the queue. Defaults to PrioritiesT.MEDIUM.
    delayed (bool, optional): True, if the queue is for delayed messages. Defaults to False.
    dead (bool, optional): True, if the queue is dead-letter.
    If set to True, takes precedence over `delayed`. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    str: queue name, representing all the arguments.

functions.name: repid.connections.redis.utils.mnc
documentation:
Message name constructor.

Args:
    key (RoutingKeyT): routing key to construct name for.
    short (bool, optional): If True, omits `m:{key.queue}:{key.priority}:` prefix.
    Defaults to False.

Returns:
    str: message name, representing all the arguments.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.ConsumerT.start
documentation: Start to consume messages from the queue.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.ConsumerT.pause
documentation: Pause message consumption.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.ConsumerT.unpause
documentation: Unpause message consumption.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.ConsumerT.finish
documentation:
Finish to consume messages.
Reject all messages (if any),
which are currently related to this consumer.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.ConsumerT.consume
documentation: Consume one message. Consumer must be started.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.get_consumer
documentation:
Consumes messages from the specified queue.
If topics are specified, each message will be checked for compliance with one of the topics.
Otherwise every message from the queue can be consumed.
Consumer should inform the broker that job execution is started.
Some brokers can adjust their load based on max_unacked_messages parameter.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.enqueue
documentation: Appends the message to the queue.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.reject
documentation: Informs message broker that job needs to be rescheduled on another worker.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.ack
documentation: Informs message broker that job execution succeed.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.nack
documentation: Informs message broker that job execution failed.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.requeue
documentation: Re-queues the message with payload/parameters. Routing key must stay the same.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.queue_declare
documentation: Creates the specified queue.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.queue_flush
documentation: Empties the queue. Doesn't delete the queue itself.

methods.name: repid.connections.abc.MessageBrokerT.queue_delete
documentation: Deletes the queue with all of its messages.

variables.name: repid.logger.logger

methods.name: repid.middlewares.middleware.Middleware.add_subscriber
documentation:
Wraps specified callable into an async function with auto kwargs mapping.
Adds the wrapped function to a list of signal subscribers.

Args:
    fn (Callable): callable to subscribe. Can be both an async or a normal function.
    Must be named correspondingly to a signal. Shouldn't contain any heavy computation.

methods.name: repid.middlewares.middleware.Middleware.add_middleware
documentation:
Scans middleware for functions and tries to subscribe them to signals.

Args:
    middleware (Any): class or class instance, containing functions or methods,
    which can be subscribed.

methods.name: repid.middlewares.middleware.Middleware.emit_signal
documentation:
Emit a signal = call every corresponding subscriber.

Args:
    name (str): name of the signal.
    kwargs (dict[str, Any]): arguments, which were initially supplied to the emitter.

functions.name: repid.middlewares.wrapper.middleware_wrapper
documentation:
Decorator for adding middleware signals emitting to a function.
Can be used as a normal function and as bracket-less/-full decorator.

Args:
    fn (Callable[FnP, Coroutine[Any, Any, FnR]] | None, optional):
        Any async function. Optional if you use the function as a decorator. Positional-only.
    name (str | None, optional):
        Name, that will be used to call middleware signals. Defaults to name of the function.

Returns:
    Callable[FnP, Coroutine[Any, Any, FnR]]:
        Function with the same signature as the initial one with middleware signal calls
        added before and after the call of the initial function. For the middleware to be
        actually called you have to supply `_repid_signal_emitter` after initialization.

classes.name: repid.connection.Connection

methods.name: repid.connection.Connection.connect
documentation: Connect to all set brokers. Marks connection as open.

methods.name: repid.connection.Connection.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect from all set brokers. Marks connection as closed.

methods.name: repid.connection.Connection._ab
documentation:
For internal use.

Shortcut to get args bucket broker or raise an error, if it isn't set.

methods.name: repid.connection.Connection._rb
documentation:
For internal use.

Shortcut to get result bucket broker or raise an error, if it isn't set.

methods.name: repid.connection.Connection.__post_init__
documentation:
Magic method that is called after the initialization of the object.

If `results_bucket_broker` is not `None`, it tries to create an instance of the
`BUCKET_CLASS` with some dummy data to check if it is compatible with the `ResultBucketT` class.
If it is not compatible, it raises a `ValueError`.

It then sets middleware signal emitter for every submitted broker.

methods.name: repid.connection.Connection._update_from_config
documentation:
For internal use.

Manually update all brokers from the current Config.
Connection dataclass represents a combination of connections to a message broker and bucket brokers.

methods.name: repid.router.Router.actor
documentation:
Actor decorator.

Args:
    name (str | None, optional):
        actor's name.
        Used for routing a message to this actor (message.topic == actor.name).
        Defaults to the name of your wrapped function.
    queue (str | None, optional):
        queue that actor will receive messages from.
        Defaults to `Router.defaults.queue`.
    retry_policy (RetryPolicyT | None, optional):
        decides for how long we should delay retry of a message.
        Defaults to `Router.defaults.retry_policy`.
    run_in_process (bool | None, optional):
        If True, runs synchronous actors inside of a `ProcessPoolExecutor`
        instead of the default `ThreadPoolExecutor`.
        Defaults to `Router.defaults.run_in_process`.
    converter (type[ConverterT] | None, optional):
        Class that decides how the arguments and return type
        should be validated & parsed.
        Defaults to `Router.defaults.converter`.

Returns:
    YourFunc: your initial function.

variables.name: repid.converter.FnR
documentation: noqa: PLC0105

methods.name: repid.converter.DefaultConverter.__init__
documentation: pretend to be an implementation of ConverterT

functions.name: repid.retry_policy.default_retry_policy_factory
documentation:
Creates default (exponential) retry policy.

Args:
    min_backoff (int, optional): Minimum amount of seconds to delay for. Defaults to 10.
    max_backoff (int, optional): Maximum amount of seconds to delay for. Defaults to 86400.
    multiplier (int, optional): Amount of seconds which will be multiplied. Defaults to 5.
    max_exponent (int, optional): Exponent can't exceed this number. Defaults to 15.

Returns:
    RetryPolicyT: exponential retry policy.

methods.name: repid.retry_policy.RetryPolicyT.__call__
documentation:
Calculates backoff for retries.

Args:
    retry_number (int, optional): what is the number of this retry.
    Always >= 1. Defaults to 1.

Returns:
    timedelta: time to wait before retring.
